metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verify command to AC line 2 that confirms absence of the old placeholder, e.g. `! grep -qi 'runbook to follow' docs/adr/0047-retire-autolander-rebase-if-necessary.md`.
findings.finding: AC line 2 declares 'ADR 0047 no longer contains the literal "runbook to follow"' as a criterion, but the verify commands provided only check for the PRESENCE of the new link — no grep (e.g. `grep -qv` or `! grep -q`) is given to confirm the ABSENCE of the old placeholder text. The criterion is stated but not verifiable by the listed commands.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC line 2: `AND ! grep -qi 'runbook to follow' docs/adr/0047-retire-autolander-rebase-if-necessary.md` exits 0 (i.e. the literal is absent after the edit).
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note to the 'identify the culprit change' section of the runbook explaining that `test.yml`'s `cancel-in-progress: true` means a red run may be attached to a later commit's SHA rather than the actual culprit — instruct the operator to inspect the full `main` commit graph (`git log --oneline origin/main`) and check whether any earlier CI run was cancelled, to correctly identify the offending change before reverting.
findings.finding: The `test.yml` workflow uses `cancel-in-progress: true` on `group: test-${{ github.ref }}`, so when two commits land on `main` in quick succession the first push's CI run is cancelled and the red result appears on the second commit's run — not the culprit's. The plan instructs the runbook to cover 'identify the culprit change' but does not account for this edge case, leaving a practitioner following the runbook without guidance when the red run is attributed to an innocent second commit.
findings.location: What section, item (b): 'identify the culprit change' / .github/workflows/test.yml lines 27-29 (concurrency + cancel-in-progress)
findings.suggested_fix: In the runbook's culprit-identification step, add: 'Note: if two changes landed rapidly, GitHub may show the red run on the second commit — check the Actions tab for any cancelled runs on the same `main` push sequence. Use `git log --oneline origin/main` to list recent commits and correlate with run timestamps to isolate the true culprit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default for the CONTRIBUTING.md placement: e.g. 'if the post-merge safety-net note does not already exist, add a new "Post-merge safety net" subsection under the CI section and link the runbook from it'.
findings.finding: The What section says cross-link 'from CONTRIBUTING.md's post-merge safety-net note', implying a pre-existing named section; if that section does not yet exist the executor must decide whether to create it or embed the link elsewhere — the AC only checks `grep -q 'infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md' CONTRIBUTING.md` (any location), so there is a defaultable gap between the instructed location and the AC.
findings.location: What bullet 2: 'Cross-link the runbook from ... CONTRIBUTING.md's post-merge safety-net note'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical: 'from CONTRIBUTING.md's post-merge safety-net note (create the subsection if absent, under the CI/workflow section)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that CONTRIBUTING.md's 'post-merge safety-net note' does not yet exist and must be CREATED (not just linked into) as part of this task — specify what section/location in CONTRIBUTING.md should host the new note.
findings.finding: The plan instructs cross-linking 'from CONTRIBUTING.md's post-merge safety-net note', implying an existing anchor note in CONTRIBUTING.md, but no such note exists there today. CONTRIBUTING.md has no mention of 'post-merge safety net', 'post-merge CI', or the revert runbook topic. This is an unbacked codebase assertion: the cross-link target does not pre-exist, so the task silently requires CREATING the safety-net note in CONTRIBUTING.md rather than merely adding a link to existing text — work that is not explicitly scoped.
findings.location: What section, second bullet: 'Cross-link the runbook from … CONTRIBUTING.md's post-merge safety-net note'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'from CONTRIBUTING.md's post-merge safety-net note' with 'by adding a post-merge safety-net note to CONTRIBUTING.md (e.g. in §2e or as a new §2f after the Submit section) that summarises the detector, links to the runbook, and notes that auto-revert is not built.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the 'post-merge safety-net note' in CONTRIBUTING.md is expected to be added by sibling ticket 5e7a before this ticket runs, OR whether this ticket is responsible for adding it; add an explicit sequencing note or expand the What section to include adding that note if it doesn't exist.
findings.finding: The plan says to cross-link the runbook 'from CONTRIBUTING.md's post-merge safety-net note', implying that note already exists in CONTRIBUTING.md. It does not — CONTRIBUTING.md currently describes the old FFO/auto-lander model (§2e, §6) with no 'post-merge safety-net' section. The plan silently depends on sibling ticket 5e7a (docs-rewrite, migration step 4) having added that section before this ticket runs. If 5e7a has not landed, there is no named anchor to link from, and this ticket's AC2 (`grep -q "infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md" CONTRIBUTING.md`) gives no guidance on WHERE in CONTRIBUTING.md the link must appear.
findings.location: What section — second bullet; AC line 2 (`grep -q "infra/runbooks/main-red-post-merge.md" CONTRIBUTING.md`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note: 'If ticket 5e7a has not yet landed, this ticket must also add a brief post-merge safety-net paragraph to CONTRIBUTING.md §2e (or a new §2f) before inserting the cross-link.' Alternatively, declare 5e7a as a `depends_on` prerequisite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact invocation of `scripts/check_docs_index.py` (run `python scripts/check_docs_index.py --help` and record the correct flag) and update AC3 to use the verified argv.
findings.finding: AC3 references `python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check` but the plan does not confirm this script accepts a `--check` flag; if the script's argv interface differs (e.g. no flags, or `--verify`), the AC silently passes or fails for the wrong reason — this is VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT #3 (wrong helper-script argv).
